(29) Learning Hexo from Scratch - Tricks: Quickly Upload Images to imgur
This post is about uploading images to imgur quickly via VSCode.

This series covers Imgur, but Imgur started blocking image uploads from Taiwan IPs as of May 16, 2025. If you're in Taiwan, you may not be able to upload to Imgur — consider switching to another image host.
(9) Learning Hexo from Scratch - Picking an Image Host
" I mentioned imgur is great but has some issues — namely that "they don't allow you to hotlink images, i.e. directly copy the image URL".
But honestly... I've got about two thousand-something images sitting on imgur, so... yeah, awkward.

If you don't care too much about the long-term safety of your images, imgur really is a great free, long-running image host. I personally mix it in when writing blog posts. This chapter was originally going to be lumped together with the previous one, but it got too long, so I split it out. We're going to pair imgur with a really handy VSCode extension that uploads images and generates the URL automatically (so convenient).
, register, and log in. Then go to this URL to apply for an
Fill in the fields in the red box:
Authorization type: OAuth 2 authorization without a callback URL
If you lose them, you'll have to re-apply. If you only forget the Client ID, you can find it under Setting -> Applications.
Now install the VSCode extension that uploads to imgur. Search the extension marketplace for "
from imgur — without these, the extension can't upload.
. In newer VSCode versions, click the gear icon in the bottom-left, then the second icon in the top-right:
There's actually one more step for the first upload. Find any image, right-click and copy the image (you read that right), then come back to VSCode and hit Ctrl + Alt + V. You'll see this window:

Don't close that window. Go back to your open Imgur tab — you should see a prompt saying vscode-imgur wants access. Click Allow. You'll get a PIN code — paste that back into VSCode. Once you've done that, you can upload images straight to Imgur from now on.
There are other extensions for the same thing:
I've only used vscode-imgur, so I didn't cover the others. But Markdown Image looks pretty solid from a quick browse, so if vscode-imgur isn't your thing, any of these three are worth trying.
The Imgur setup flow should be roughly the same.
That's it for this Ironman post. Two posts left, somehow...
